title: Stop Prompting. Start Looping (The New AI Way)
summary: Loop engineering replaces manual prompt iteration by defining success criteria, letting an agent act, and using a judge model to verify results and repeat until the goal is met. The approach includes several implementations: Fusion loops with configurable rounds, agent Kanban boards with planner/builder/reviewer roles, Fusion boardroom combining multiple outputs, and Sakana Fugu council approaches.
writeup: The video introduces loop engineering as an alternative to manual prompt iteration: instead of refining a single prompt by hand, the method defines success criteria, lets an agent act, and uses a judge model to verify the output and repeat until the goal is met. It walks through several implementations of this doer-judge pattern, including Fusion loops with configurable rounds, agent Kanban boards that split work across planner, builder, and reviewer roles, a Fusion boardroom that combines multiple outputs, and Sakana Fugu council approaches. The takeaway is a shift from one-shot prompting toward verification-driven loops that spend more compute at run time to converge on a target.
